Official UCD pages linked between AI Governance Principles, AI Services, GenAI Teaching and Learning resources, UCD Library AI guidance, and Academic Integrity Policy.

artifacts.summary: Official UCD sources on ucd.ie, hub.ucd.ie, and libguides.ucd.ie provided AI governance, teaching and learning, academic integrity, assessment, approved-tool, privacy, and library-use guidance. Two official school or college pages were rejected for university-wide findings because their scope is limited to a college or school.

artifacts.claimText: University College Dublin publishes University Management Team-approved AI Governance Principles for institutional AI use, including human oversight, fairness, transparency, compliance, ethical design, AI literacy, stakeholder engagement, and risk assessment.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCD's faculty AI guidelines say a module descriptor must clearly state whether student AI use is permissible in the module and under what conditions.

citation.citationTitle: Faculty Guidelines for the Use of AI in Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCD guidance directs staff and students toward institutionally supported AI tools that meet University privacy and IT security standards, and warns against entering personal data into external or non-UCD licensed tools.
artifacts.normalizedValue: use institutionally supported AI tools for privacy and security; avoid personal data in external tools

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCD's Academic Integrity Policy says AI-generated content may not be submitted without explicit permission and attribution, and that generative AI outputs must be used critically, ethically, cited, and acknowledged.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI-generated content requires permission and attribution

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCD Teaching and Learning assessment guidance advises educators to be transparent with students about permitted AI use and to apply more robust approaches for high-stakes graded assessments.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UCD Library guidance for the UCD community advises users to disclose generative AI use in academic work, follow school citation style, keep records of AI interactions, and fact-check AI-generated information.
artifacts.normalizedValue: disclose, cite, record, and verify GenAI use

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim about published UCD AI governance principles, not a claim that a detailed enforceable AI policy applies to all use cases.
Approved because the source explicitly uses mandatory language for module descriptor AI-use statements.
Approved with privacy framing because evidence comes from UCD IT Services and UCD faculty guidelines; the claim does not list every tool as permanently approved.
Approved because the Academic Integrity Policy directly addresses permission, attribution, citation, and acknowledgement for GenAI content.
Approved as guidance rather than a binding rule; wording preserves the advisory nature of the assessment-design page.
Approved as UCD Library guidance for the UCD community; claim does not override module, school, or policy-specific rules.

artifacts.title: University College Dublin AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged OpenClaw review found official UCD sources for AI governance principles, faculty teaching and learning guidance, academic integrity rules for AI-generated content, assessment-design transparency guidance, IT Services privacy and tool guidance, and UCD Library advice on disclosure, citation, and verification. School or college-specific pages were recorded but excluded from university-wide findings.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
College and school specific UCD pages were not used to support university-wide conclusions.
The artifacts are staged candidates only and are not canonical public policy records.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
